title: BrainCraft Lesson
description: A single lesson in the BrainCraft curriculum. Lessons are structured data first, prose second (see docs/09-content-engine/overview.md) — the rendered lesson page is derived from this object, never authored independently of it. A lesson that fails this schema must not ship (see CLAUDE.md and docs/18-testing/overview.md).

id.description: Stable unique slug, e.g. 'python-dictionaries'. Never reused, even if a lesson is deprecated.

skillFolder.description: Must match a curriculum/ folder name exactly (docs/02-curriculum/overview.md).

missionId.description: M1 Learn Programming, M2 Manipulate Data, M3 Predict the Future, M4 Teach Machines, M5 Optimise Decisions, M6 Understand Energy, M7 Simulate a Grid, M8 Build the AI Energy OS (docs/02-curriculum/overview.md).

concept.description: Human-readable concept name, e.g. 'Dictionaries'. Should correspond to a knowledge graph node id once schemas/concept-node.schema.json exists.

knowledgeGraphNodeId.description: Optional forward reference to this concept's node id in the knowledge graph (docs/08-knowledge-graph/overview.md), once that data exists.

dependencies.description: Concept/knowledge-graph-node ids that must be at mastery threshold before this lesson is offered. May be empty only for true entry-point lessons.

whyItMatters.description: Explicit statement of why this concept matters for the AI Energy OS mission, shown near the top of the lesson (CLAUDE.md content standards). Must not be generic filler.

capstoneContribution.description: Which piece of the AI Energy OS this lesson's work feeds (docs/07-capstones/overview.md), e.g. 'Digital Twin data ingestion' or 'Reinforcement Learning Optimiser reward shaping'.

lessonFlow.description: The canonical 14-step lesson flow (docs/00-foundations/DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md). flashcards, miniProject content, and assessment are modeled as their own top-level fields below rather than nested here, since they carry structured (not narrative) data.

isBossBattle.description: True only for the less-scaffolded, synthesis-level assessment at the end of a skill folder or mission (docs/00-foundations/PROJECT_GLOSSARY.md).

labs.description: Optional — not every concept needs a hands-on lab (docs/00-foundations/DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md on avoiding manufactured interactivity).

simulations.description: Optional interactive/visual simulators this lesson embeds or links to (docs/simulations/README.md).

flashcards.description: Required — every lesson must produce at least one spaced-repetition flashcard (docs/01-learning-science/overview.md). A lesson with zero flashcards fails validation.

references.description: Required — every factual/technical claim traces to a dated, versioned source, so the Living Curriculum can detect staleness (docs/09-content-engine/overview.md). A lesson with zero references fails validation.

version.description: Incremented on every content regeneration. Learners mid-progress on an earlier version finish that version rather than being silently migrated (docs/09-content-engine/overview.md).

supersededBy.description: Lesson id that replaces this one, set when status is 'deprecated'.

lastValidatedAgainstReferences.description: Last date the living-curriculum staleness check confirmed all references are still current.

primaryType.description: Multiple-choice is intentionally excluded from primary assessment types (docs/05-assessments/overview.md: 'banned as a primary evaluation method').

supplementaryQuiz.description: Optional low-stakes multiple-choice comprehension checks — never the primary assessment.

versionOrDate.description: Library version (e.g. 'pandas 2.2') or publication date this reference pins to.
